_comment: Slice 105 strict-runtime seccomp profile (§43 Arc 5). DEFENSE-IN-DEPTH, opt-in via JARVIS_RUNTIME_SANDBOX_SECCOMP_PROFILE. Default action ALLOW with an explicit ERRNO deny-list of host-escape / privilege-escalation / kernel-module / debug syscalls that a benign Python APPLY/VERIFY payload never issues — so it hardens the jail WITHOUT risking a broken interpreter. It complements (does not replace) the active controls: --cap-drop ALL, --security-opt no-new-privileges, --network none, --read-only, --pids-limit, plus Docker's own default seccomp profile. HONEST: this profile is NOT exercised in CI (no Docker daemon on the macOS/arm64 dev host) — validate it against your production image on a Docker/GCP host before relying on it as a primary control.

syscalls.comment: host-escape / privilege / kernel-module / debug syscalls — denied; a benign python payload never issues these, so the interpreter is unaffected.
